meta.setting: Stage roads, muddy grades, way stations, and French Gulch freight schedules
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with travel, payroll risk, passenger trouble, law heat, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, stage-era road-town texture, and county travel pressure.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Gold rush communities, roads, mining settlement, and regional movement.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Stage Drivers cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented schedule note, rein warning, or grade saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Six-Horse Road Hands
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing stagecoach drivers and guards who move mail, passengers, payrolls, and stories over bad grades
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate stage drivers as professionals balancing speed, risk, passenger temper, horse health, road knowledge, and company schedules.
Tie future moves to team handling, ambush response, schedule leverage, mail secrets, road repairs, passenger triage, payroll risk, and the choice to abandon cargo.
Make the page kinetic but practical: reins, brake, wheel iron, wet leather, passenger fear, guard fatigue, and the driver's hands as the map.
Let them be allies, liabilities, rumor carriers, and scene engines for fast-changing travel.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Their hands should read as maps: rein burn, tobacco stain, and old fear at the knuckle. A stage route is a promise made to weather, horses, and thieves at the same time.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A stage scene should ask whether schedule, safety, mail, payroll, or a passenger's secret matters most when the road turns bad.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western stage and road language only.
Keep travel danger physical, social, and schedule-driven.
The generated page must include image path images/stage-drivers.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not make stage travel clean, safe, or picturesque by default
do not use modern slang, automobiles, modern roads, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about stage travel, team handling, mail, payroll, road danger, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
